channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: An obscure OS could surpass ChromeOS by the end of 2027 — Huawei's Harmony OS grows 9x in 12 months, and it's only the beginning
item.description: HarmonyOS expands from smartphones to PCs, showing tenfold growth potential, leveraging Huawei’s ecosystem, and reaching almost one billion devices.

item.title: Brandon Sanderson Explains Why He Doesn’t Write About Elves or Dwarves
item.description: The best-selling fantasy author talks about his evolving relationship with the works of J.R.R. Tolkien.

item.title: I just proved the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra screen is slightly dimmer than the S25 Ultra — but it doesn't make me love it any less
item.description: Is the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra slightly less bright than the S25 Ultra? Tests and visual inspection prove it, but it's so minor that most won't notice or care.

item.title: 'We developed a new muscle': Tim Cook on celebrating the past and why Alicia Keys 50th Anniversary concert at Grand Central was authentically Apple
item.description: Apple kicked off global, in-person 50th Anniversary celebrations with a surprise Alicia Keys concert at its iconic Grand Central store — and Tim Cook is embracing celebrating Apple's past.

item.title: 5 of my favorite horror movies to stream this Friday the 13th
item.description: Head down to Camp Crystal Lake and beyond

item.title: Not everything needs to be known: How one day with no phone changed my life
item.description: I ditched my phone for a day and now I'm sleeping better, have more patience, and feel much calmer.

item.title: The Target boycott over DEI isn’t over yet
item.description: Even as one boycott came to an end this week, other activists remain committed to the cause—and are still pressing Target to reevaluate its DEI commitments. Headlines this week signaled that a major boycott against Target had come to an end. The retail giant has been under fire since winding back many of its commitments to diversity, equity, and inclusion a year ago, which sparked widespread criticism from the Black community and consumer boycotts that had a tangible impact on the business. Over the course of 2025, Target’s already sluggish sales dropped further, and its share price fell by ov...

item.title: OpenAI reportedly plans to add Sora video generation to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI plans to add its Sora video generation model directly into ChatGPT, The Information reports . The standalone Sora app was seen as a smash hit when it launched alongside Sora 2 in September 2025, but interest in the video generation app has fallen in the time since as users ran into limits on the amount and kinds of videos they could create.Adding Sora to the ChatGPT could give the model a second life, and ideally grow the ChatGPT app's weekly active users from the 900 million OpenAI reported in February, to a billion or more. According to The Information, the standalone Sora app will st...

item.title: Child safety group blasts YouTube for million dollar gamble on AI content for kids
item.description: Google is investing $1 million in an AI-powered kid's media company. Child safety experts warn it won't solve the platform's slop problem.

item.title: ‘Divine Incursions’ Is a Brainy Monster-of-the-Week Thriller That Will Scratch Your ‘X-Files’ Itch
item.description: This detective-style sci-fi Yen Press series channels classic genre paranoia without throwing hands to solve supernatural problems.

item.title: States’ anti-monopoly case against Live Nation continues Monday
item.description: The Live Nation-Ticketmaster trial is back on. Dozens of states are expected to move forward with their claims against the company's alleged concert industry monopoly beginning on Monday, following a brief hearing on Friday. The Justice Department and a handful of states have accepted settlements with the company, but the majority of the 40 state […]...

item.title: Anthropic’s forced removal from the U.S. government is threatening critical AI nuclear safety research
item.description: As government agencies struggle to understand whether they’re allowed to use Claude, projects at the Energy Department designed to limit AI’s work on nuclear weapons may suddenly halt. The sudden wind-down of Anthropic technology within the U.S. government is raising concerns about whether federal officials, without access to Claude, might fall behind in the quest to guard against the threat of AI-generated or AI-assisted nuclear and chemical weapons. ...

item.title: Review: The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist is a panic attack, in a good way
item.description: How worried should you be about the rise of artificial intelligence? Daniel Kwan produces "The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist." Review.

item.title: Erupcja review: Is Charli XCX charting her path to movie stardom?
item.description: Charli XCX, the pop star behind "brat summer" headlines a sapphic romance with "Erupcja." Review.

item.title: AI will power Fandom from spectator to co-star
item.description: AI is enabling fan experiences that are persistent, co-created, and reciprocal. For decades, the relationship between a fan and a content franchise was defined by scarcity: You watched a movie or binged a season, then had to wait months or years for the next installment. Fans were spectators, limited to going to the concert, game, or movie, buying merch, and tweeting about their favorite moments....

item.title: Digg lays off staff and shuts down app as company retools
item.description: Digg laid off a significant number of staff and shut down its app, but says it's not giving up on the startup.

item.title: Pentagon Begins Search for Remains of WWII POWs Lost on Japanese ‘Hell Ship’
item.description: A team of 15 specialist divers dove into waters off the coast of the Philippines last month—initiating a forensic recovery operation that one Pentagon scientist said could take "months or years."

item.title: Spotify tests letting users directly customize their Taste Profile
item.description: Spotify Premium users in New Zealand will be the first to experience the service's latest personalization feature. The company is letting users view and make changes to their Taste Profile directly. If your algorithm is serving up too much Bieber, you can politely tell the algorithm to ease up. Beta testers will be able to […]

item.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
item.description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons.
